page: Microsoft Azure EC48ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU runs at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is supported by 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage is bundled, providing 1843 GB of capacity with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and single-core computational capabilities. The instance achieved top 25% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as the Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory performance is also notable, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25%, though memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall within the average range. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates top 10% performance in text generation for small (135M) LLM models, alongside strong performance in ray tracing and image processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC48ads_cc_v5 offers a dense allocation of memory and local storage relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for resource-intensive workloads without requiring dedicated GPU hardware. However, memory bandwidth remains in the average tier, which may present a bottleneck for memory-bound sequential operations. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, software compilation, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure EC48ads_cc_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 1843 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Benchmark data shows strong performance in multi-core processing, Passmark memory tests, and small-model LLM text generation, where it reaches the top 10% tier. Conversely, memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier. This server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-dense workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and software compilation.
